metadata.summary: Python package for Microwave Radiometer processing in ACTRIS

documentation:
# MWRpy

[![MWRpy tests](https://github.com/actris-cloudnet/mwrpy/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/actris-cloudnet/mwrpy/actions/workflows/test.yml)
[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/mwrpy.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/mwrpy)
[![DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/619528669.svg)](https://zenodo.org/doi/10.5281/zenodo.11614185)
[![paper](https://joss.theoj.org/papers/10.21105/joss.06733/status.svg)](https://doi.org/10.21105/joss.06733)

MWRpy is a Python software to process RPG Microwave Radiometer data and is developed
at the University of Cologne, Germany as part
of the [Aerosol, Clouds and Trace Gases Research Infrastructure (ACTRIS)](https://actris.eu/).

The software features reading raw data, Level 1 quality control, generation of
Level 2 data products and visualization and is based on the IDL code
[mwr_pro](https://zenodo.org/records/7973553).

The netCDF data format including metadata information, variable names and file naming
is designed to be compliant with the data structure and naming convention
developed in the [EUMETNET Profiling Programme E-PROFILE](https://www.eumetnet.eu/).

MWRpy documentation: <https://actris-cloudnet.github.io/mwrpy/>

![MWRpy example output](https://atmos.meteo.uni-koeln.de/~hatpro/quicklooks/obs/site/jue/tophat/actris/level2/2022/10/29/20221029_juelich_temperature.png)

## Citing

If you wish to acknowledge MWRpy in your publication, please cite:

> Marke et al., (2024). MWRpy: A Python package for processing microwave radiometer data. Journal of Open Source
> Software, 9(98), 6733, https://doi.org/10.21105/joss.06733

## Installation

From PyPI:

```shell
python3 -m pip install mwrpy
```

From GitHub:

```shell
git clone https://github.com/actris-cloudnet/mwrpy.git
cd mwrpy
python3 -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate
pip3 install --upgrade pip
pip3 install .
```

MWRpy requires Python 3.10 or newer.

## Configuration

The folder `mwrpy/site_config/` contains configuration files for each instrument
type, which defines the input and output data paths etc.
For example, this is the [configuration file for RPG-HATPRO](mwrpy/site_config/hatpro.yaml).

The folders for each site, e.g. `mwrpy/site_config/hyytiala/`, contain a
site and instrument specific configuration file (`config.yaml`) and retrieval coefficients.
For example, this is the [configuration file for Hyytiälä](mwrpy/site_config/hyytiala/config.yaml).

## Command line usage

MWRpy can be run using the command line tool `mwrpy/cli.py`:

    usage: mwrpy/cli.py [-h] -s SITE [-d YYYY-MM-DD] [--start YYYY-MM-DD]
                           [--stop YYYY-MM-DD] [-p ...] [{process,plot}]

Arguments:

| Short | Long         | Default             | Description                                                                        |
| :---- | :----------- | :------------------ | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `-h`  | `--help`     |                     | Show help and exit.                                                                |
| `-s`  | `--site`     |                     | Site to process data from, e.g, `hyytiala`. Required.                              |
| `-d`  | `--date`     |                     | Single date to be processed. Alternatively, `--start` and `--stop` can be defined. |
|       | `--start`    | `current day - 1`   | Starting date.                                                                     |
|       | `--stop`     | `current day `      | Stopping date.                                                                     |
| `-p`  | `--products` | 1C01, single, multi | Processed products, e.g, `1C01, 2I02, 2P03, single`, see below.                    |

Commands:

| Command     | Description                                                |
| :---------- | :--------------------------------------------------------- |
| `process`   | Process data and generate plots (default).                 |
| `plot`      | Only generate plots.                                       |
| `no-plot`   | Only generate products.                                    |
| `reprocess` | Like `process`, but skips days when data processing fails. |

### Data types

#### Level 1

- 1B01: MWR brightness temperatures from .BRT and .BLB/.BLS files + retrieved spectrum
- 1B11: IR brightness temperatures from .IRT files
- 1B21: Weather station data from .MET files
- 1C01: Combined data type with time corresponding to 1B01

#### Level 2

- 2I01: Liquid water path (LWP)
- 2I02: Integrated water vapor (IWV)
- 2I06: Stability Indices
- 2P01: Temperature profiles from single-pointing observations
- 2P02: Temperature profiles from multiple-pointing observations
- 2P03: Absolute humidity profiles
- 2P04: Relative humidity profiles (derived from 2P01/2P02 + 2P03)
- 2P07: Potential temperature (derived from 2P01/2P02 + 2P03)
- 2P08: Equivalent potential temperature (derived from 2P01/2P02 + 2P03)
- single: Single pointing data product (including 2I01, 2I02, 2I06, 2P01, 2P03, and derived products)
- multi: Multiple pointing data product (including 2P02, and derived products)

## Licence

MIT

Initializes an RPG MWR file for writing.

Args:

: file_name: File name to be generated.
  dimensions: Dictionary containing dimension for this file.
  rpg_arrays: Dictionary containing {class}`RpgArray` instances.
  att_global: Dictionary containing site specific global attributes

classes.name: mwrpy.rpg_mwr.RpgArray

methods.name: mwrpy.rpg_mwr.RpgArray.fetch_attributes
documentation: Returns list of user-defined attributes.

methods.name: mwrpy.rpg_mwr.RpgArray.set_attributes
documentation: Overwrites existing instance attributes.

Stores netCDF4 variables, numpy arrays and scalars as RpgArrays.

Args:

: variable: The netCDF4 {class}`Variable` instance,
  numpy array (masked or regular), or scalar (float, int).
  name: Name of the variable.
  units_from_user: Units of the variable.

Attributes:

: name (str): Name of the variable.
  data (ndarray): The actual data.
  data_type (str): 'i4' for integers, 'f4' for floats.
  units (str): The `units_from_user` argument if it is given. Otherwise

  > copied from the original netcdf4 variable. Empty if input is just data.

classes.name: mwrpy.rpg_mwr.Rpg

methods.name: mwrpy.rpg_mwr.Rpg.find_valid_times
documentation: Sorts timestamps and finds valid times.
Base class for RPG MWR.

modules.name: mwrpy.utils
documentation: Module for general helper functions.

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.seconds2hours
documentation:
Converts seconds since some epoch to fraction hour.

Args:
    time_in_seconds: 1-D array of seconds since some epoch that starts on midnight.

Returns:
    Time as fraction hour.

Notes:
    Excludes leap seconds.

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.epoch2unix
documentation:
Converts seconds since some epoch to Unix time in UTC.

Args:
    epoch_time: 1-D array of seconds since the given epoch.
    time_ref: HATPRO time reference (1: UTC, 0: Local Time)
    epoch: Epoch of the input time. Default is (2001,1,1,0,0,0).

Returns:
    ndarray: Unix time in seconds since (1970,1,1,0,0,0).

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.isscalar
documentation:
Tests if input is scalar.

By "scalar" we mean that array has a single value.

Examples:
    >>> isscalar(1)
        True
    >>> isscalar([1])
        True
    >>> isscalar(np.array(1))
        True
    >>> isscalar(np.array([1]))
        True

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.isbit
documentation:
Tests if nth bit (0,1,2..) is set.

Args:
    array: Integer array.
    nth_bit: Investigated bit.

Returns:
    Boolean array denoting values where nth_bit is set.

Raises:
    ValueError: negative bit as input.

Examples:
    >>> isbit(np.array([4, 5]), 1)
        array([False, False])
    >>> isbit(np.array([4, 5]), 2)
        array([ True,  True])

See Also:
    utils.setbit()

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.setbit
documentation:
Sets nth bit (0, 1, 2..) on number.

Args:
    array: Integer array.
    nth_bit: Bit to be set.

Returns:
    Integer where nth bit is set.

Raises:
    ValueError: negative bit as input.

Examples:
    >>> setbit(np.array([0, 1]), 1)
        array([2, 3])
    >>> setbit(np.array([0, 1]), 2)
        array([4, 5])

See Also:
    utils.isbit()

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.interpol_2d
documentation:
Interpolates 2-D data in one dimension.

Args:
    x_in: 1-D array with shape (n,).
    array: 2-D input data with shape (n, m).
    x_new: 1-D target vector with shape (N,).

Returns:
    array: Interpolated data with shape (N, m).

Notes:
    0-values are masked in the returned array.

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.interpolate_2d
documentation:
Linear interpolation of gridded 2d data.

Args:
    x: 1-D array.
    y: 1-D array.
    z: 2-D array at points (x, y).
    x_new: 1-D array.
    y_new: 1-D array.

Returns:
    Interpolated data.

Notes:
    Does not work with nans. Ignores mask of masked data. Does not extrapolate.

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.add_interpol1d
documentation:
Adds interpolated 1d field to dict, supporting masked arrays.

Args:
    data0: Output dict.
    data1: Input field to be added & interpolated. Supports masked arrays.
    time1: Time of input field.
    output_name: Name of output field.

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.seconds2date
documentation:
Converts seconds since some epoch to datetime (UTC).

Args:
    time_in_seconds: Seconds since some epoch.
    epoch: Epoch, default is (1970, 1, 1) (UTC).

Returns:
    [year, month, day, hours, minutes, seconds] formatted as '05' etc (UTC).

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.str_to_numeric
documentation: Converts string to number (int or float).

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.add_time_bounds
documentation: Adds time bounds.

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.get_coeff_list
documentation: Returns list of .nc coefficient file(s).

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.get_file_list
documentation: Returns file list for specified path.

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.update_lev1_attributes
documentation: Removes attributes that are not needed for specified Level 1 data type.

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.read_nc_field_name

Reads selected variable name from a netCDF file.

Args:
    nc_file: netCDF file name.
    name: Variable to be read, e.g. 'temperature'.

Returns:
    str

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.read_nc_fields
documentation:
Reads selected variables from a netCDF file.

Args:
    nc_file: netCDF file name.
    name: Variable to be read, e.g. 'lwp'.

Returns:
    np.ndarray

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.append_data
documentation:
Appends data to a dictionary field (creates the field if not yet present).

Args:
    data_in: Dictionary where data will be appended.
    key: Key of the field.
    array: Numpy array to be appended to data_in[key].

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.convolve2DFFT
documentation:
2D convolution using fft.
<slab>: 2d array, with optional mask.
<kernel>: 2d array, convolution kernel.
<max_missing>: real, max tolerable percentage of missing within any
               convolution window.
               E.g. if <max_missing> is 0.5, when over 50% of values
               within a given element are missing, the center will be
               set as missing (<res>=0, <resmask>=1). If only 40% is
               missing, center value will be computed using the remaining
               60% data in the element.
               NOTE that out-of-bound grids are counted as missing, this
               is different from convolve2D(), where the number of valid
               values at edges drops as the kernel approaches the edge.
Return <result>: 2d convolution.

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.date_string_to_date
documentation: Convert YYYY-MM-DD to Python date.

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.get_time
documentation: Returns current UTC-time.

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.get_date_from_past
documentation:
Return date N-days ago.

Args:
    n: Number of days to skip (can be negative, when it means the future).
    reference_date: Date as "YYYY-MM-DD". Default is the current date.

Returns:
    str: Date as "YYYY-MM-DD".

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.get_processing_dates
documentation: Returns processing dates.

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.date_range
documentation: Returns range between two dates (datetimes).

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.copy_variables
documentation:
Copies variables (and their attributes) from one file to another.

Args:

    target: Target object.

functions.name: mwrpy.utils.copy_global
documentation:
Copies global attributes from one file to another.

Args:

    target: Target object.
    attributes: List of attributes to be copied.

modules.name: mwrpy.plots.plot_utils

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.plot_utils._get_ret_flag
documentation: Returns quality flag for frequencies used in retrieval.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.plot_utils._get_lev1
documentation: Returns name of lev1 file.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.plot_utils._get_freq_flag
documentation: Returns array of flag values for each frequency.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.plot_utils._get_bit_flag
documentation: Returns array of flag values for each bit.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.plot_utils._get_unmasked_values
documentation: Returns unmasked time and data.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.plot_utils._nan_time_gaps
documentation: Finds time gaps bigger than 5min (default) and inserts nan.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.plot_utils._gap_array
documentation:
Returns edges of time gaps bigger than 5min (default).
End of gap for current day is current time.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.plot_utils._calculate_rolling_mean
documentation: Returns rolling mean.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.plot_utils._read_location
documentation: Returns site name.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.plot_utils.heatmap
documentation:
Create a heatmap from a numpy array and two lists of labels.

Parameters
----------
data
    A 2D numpy array of shape (M, N).
row_labels
    A list or array of length M with the labels for the rows.
col_labels
    A list or array of length N with the labels for the columns.
ax
    A `matplotlib.axes.Axes` instance to which the heatmap is plotted.  If
    not provided, use current axes or create a new one.  Optional.
cbar_kw
    A dictionary with arguments to `matplotlib.Figure.colorbar`.  Optional.
cbarlabel
    The label for the colorbar.  Optional.
**kwargs
    All other arguments are forwarded to `imshow`.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.plot_utils.annotate_heatmap
documentation:
A function to annotate a heatmap.

Parameters
----------
im
    The AxesImage to be labeled.
data
    Data used to annotate.  If None, the image's data is used.  Optional.
valfmt
    The format of the annotations inside the heatmap.  This should either
    use the string format method, e.g. "$ {x:.2f}", or be a
    `matplotlib.ticker.Formatter`.  Optional.
textcolors
    A pair of colors.  The first is used for values below a threshold,
    the second for those above.  Optional.
threshold
    Value in data units according to which the colors from textcolors are
    applied.  If None (the default) uses the middle of the colormap as
    separation.  Optional.
**kwargs
    All other arguments are forwarded to each call to `text` used to create
    the text labels.

modules.name: mwrpy.plots.plot_meta
documentation: Module for metadata of plotting module.

classes.name: mwrpy.plots.plot_meta.PlotMeta

modules.name: mwrpy.constants

modules.name: mwrpy.level1.met_quality_control
documentation: Module for meteorological sensor quality control.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.met_quality_control.apply_met_qc
documentation:
This function performs quality control of meteorological sensor data.

Args:
    data: Level 1 data.
    params: Site specific parameters.

Returns:
    None

Raises:
    RuntimeError:

Example:
    from level1.met_quality_control import apply_met_qc
    apply_met_qc('lev1_data', 'params')

modules.name: mwrpy.level1.lev1_meta_nc
documentation: Module for Level 1 Metadata.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.lev1_meta_nc.get_data_attributes
documentation:
Adds Metadata for RPG MWR Level 1 variables for NetCDF file writing.

Args:
    rpg_variables: RpgArray instances.
    data_type: Data type of the netCDF file.

Returns:
    Dictionary

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Specified data type is not supported.

Example:
    from level1.lev1_meta_nc import get_data_attributes
    att = get_data_attributes('data','data_type')

modules.name: mwrpy.level1.rpg_bin
documentation: This module contains all functions to read in RPG MWR binary files.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.rpg_bin.stack_files
documentation: This function calls extension specific reader and stacks data and header.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.rpg_bin.read_bls
documentation: This function reads RPG MWR .BLS binary files.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.rpg_bin.read_brt
documentation: Reads BRT files and returns header and data as dictionary.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.rpg_bin.read_blb
documentation: Reads BLB files and returns header and data as dictionary.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.rpg_bin.read_irt
documentation: Reads IRT files and returns header and data as dictionary.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.rpg_bin.read_hkd

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.rpg_bin.read_met
documentation: Reads MET files and returns header and data as dictionary.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.rpg_bin._decode_angles
documentation:
Decode elevation and azimuth angles.

```pycon
>>> _decode_angles(np.array([1267438.5]), method=1)
(array([138.5]), array([267.4]))
>>> _decode_angles(np.array([1453031045, -900001232]), method=2)
(array([145.3, -90. ]), array([310.45,  12.32]))
```

Based on `interpret_angle` from mwr_raw2l1 licensed under BSD 3-Clause:
<https://github.com/MeteoSwiss/mwr_raw2l1/blob/0738490d22f77138cdf9329bf102f319c78be584/mwr_raw2l1/readers/reader_rpg_helpers.py#L30>

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.rpg_bin._fix_header
documentation: Maybe can get rid of this function later.

classes.name: mwrpy.level1.rpg_bin.RpgBin
documentation: Class for RPG binary files.

modules.name: mwrpy.level1.quality_control
documentation: Quality control for level1 data.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.quality_control.apply_qc
documentation:
This function performs the quality control of level 1 data.

Args:
    site: Name of site.
    data_in: Level 1 data.
    params: Site specific parameters.
    coeff_files: Retrieval coefficients.

Returns:
    None

Raises:
    RuntimeError:

Example:
    from level1.quality_control import apply_qc
    apply_qc('site', 'lev1_data', 'params')

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.quality_control.orbpos
documentation:
Calculates sun & moon elevation/azimuth angles
and returns index for observations in the direction of the sun.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.quality_control.spectral_consistency
documentation:
Applies spectral consistency coefficients for given frequency index,
writes 2S02 product and returns indices to be flagged.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.quality_control.ele_retrieval
documentation: Extracts elevation angles used in retrieval.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.quality_control.rm_retrieval
documentation: Extracts retrieval uncertainty.

modules.name: mwrpy.level1.write_lev1_nc
documentation: Module for writing Level 1 netCDF files.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.write_lev1_nc.lev1_to_nc
documentation:
This function reads one day of RPG MWR binary files,
adds attributes and writes it into netCDF file.

Args:
    data_type: Data type of the netCDF file.
    path_to_files: Folder containing one day of RPG MWR binary files.
    site: Name of site.
    output_file: Output file name.
    coeff_files: List of coefficient files.
    instrument_config: Dictionary containing information about the instrument.

Raises:
    MissingInputData: if required input file is missing.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.write_lev1_nc.prepare_data
documentation: Load and prepare data for netCDF writing.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.write_lev1_nc._append_hkd
documentation: Append hkd data on same time grid and perform TB sanity check.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.write_lev1_nc.hkd_sanity_check
documentation: Perform sanity checks for .HKD data.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.write_lev1_nc._find_closest_ind
documentation: Find indices of the closest values in two arrays within a threshold.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.write_lev1_nc._add_bls
documentation: Add BLS boundary-layer scans using a linear time axis.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.write_lev1_nc._add_blb
documentation: Add BLB boundary-layer scans using a linear time axis.

functions.name: mwrpy.level1.write_lev1_nc._azi_correction
documentation: Azimuth correction (transform to "geographical" coordinates).

modules.name: mwrpy.process_mwrpy
documentation: Module for processing.

modules.name: mwrpy.level2.lev2_meta_nc
documentation: Module for Level 2 Metadata.

functions.name: mwrpy.level2.lev2_meta_nc.get_data_attributes
documentation:
Adds Metadata for RPG MWR Level 2 variables for NetCDF file writing.

Args:
    rpg_variables: RpgArray instances.
    data_type: Data type of the netCDF file.
    coeff: Coefficient data of variable

Returns:
    Dictionary

Raises:
    RuntimeError: Specified data type is not supported.

Example:
    from level2.lev2_meta_nc import get_data_attributes
    att = get_data_attributes('data','data_type')

modules.name: mwrpy.level2.get_ret_coeff
documentation: Module to load in retrieval coefficient files.

functions.name: mwrpy.level2.get_ret_coeff.get_mvr_coeff
documentation:
This function extracts retrieval coefficients for given files.

Args:
    coeff_files: List of coefficient files.
    site: Name of site.
    prefix: Identifier for type of product.
    freq: Frequencies of observations.

Examples:
    >>> from mwrpy.level2.get_ret_coeff import get_mvr_coeff
    >>> get_mvr_coeff('site_name', 'lwp', np.array([22, 31.4]))

functions.name: mwrpy.level2.get_ret_coeff.read_coeff_ascii
documentation: Reads the coefficients from an ascii file.

modules.name: mwrpy.level2.lwp_offset
documentation: Module for LWP offset correction.

functions.name: mwrpy.level2.lwp_offset.correct_lwp_offset
documentation:
This function corrects Lwp offset using the
2min Lwp standard deviation and the water vapor
channel as proxy for a humidity dependent threshold.

Args:
    lev1: Level 1 data.
    lwp_org: Lwp array.
    index: Index to use.

modules.name: mwrpy.plots.generate_plots
documentation: Module for plotting.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.generate_plots.generate_figure
documentation:
Generates a mwrpy figure.

Args:
    nc_file (str): Input file.
    field_names (list): Variable names to be plotted.
    show (bool, optional): If True, shows the figure. Default is True.
    save_path (str, optional): Setting this path will save the figure (in the
        given path). Default is None, when the figure is not saved.
    max_y (int, optional): Upper limit in the plots (km). Default is 12.
    ele_range (tuple, optional): Range of elevation angles to be plotted.
    pointing (int, optional): Type of observation (0: single pointing, 1: BL scan)
    dpi (int, optional): Figure quality (if saved). Higher value means
        more pixels, i.e., better image quality. Default is 120.
    image_name (str, optional): Name (and full path) of the output image.
        Overrides the *save_path* option. Default is None.
    sub_title (bool, optional): Add subtitle to image. Default is True.
    title (bool, optional): Add title to image. Default is True.

Returns:
    Dimensions of the generated figure in pixels.
    File name of the generated figure.

Examples:
    >>> from mwrpy.plots import generate_figure
    >>> generate_figure('lev2_file.nc', ['lwp'])

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.generate_plots._mark_gaps
documentation: Mark gaps in time and data.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.generate_plots.handle_saving
documentation: Returns file name of plot.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.generate_plots._set_labels
documentation: Sets labels and returns date of netCDF file.

documentation: Returns time and height arrays.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.generate_plots._read_time_vector
documentation: Converts time vector to fraction hour.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.generate_plots._screen_high_altitudes
documentation:
Removes altitudes from 2D data that are not visible in the figure.
Bug in pcolorfast causing effect to axis not noticing limitation while
saving fig. This fixes that bug till pcolorfast does fixing themselves.

Args:
    data_field (ndarray): 2D data array.
    ax_values (tuple): Time and height 1D arrays.
    max_y (int): Upper limit in the plots (km).

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.generate_plots._set_ax
documentation: Sets ticks and tick labels for plt.imshow().

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.generate_plots._get_standard_time_ticks
documentation: Returns typical ticks / labels for a time vector between 0-24h.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.generate_plots._init_colorbar
documentation: Returns colorbar.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.generate_plots._read_date
documentation: Returns measurement date.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.generate_plots._add_subtitle

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.generate_plots._get_subtitle_text
documentation: Returns string with site name and date.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.generate_plots._create_save_name
documentation: Creates file name for saved images.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.generate_plots._plot_segment_data
documentation:
Plots categorical 2D variable.

Args:
    ax (obj): Axes object of subplot (1,2,3,.. [1,1,],[1,2]... etc.)
    data (ndarray): 2D data array.
    name (string): Name of plotted data.
    axes (tuple): Time and height 1D arrays.
    nc_file (str): Input file.

functions.name: mwrpy.plots.generate_plots._plot_colormesh_data
documentation:
Plots continuous 2D variable.
Creates only one plot, so can be used both one plot and subplot type of figs.

Args:
    ax (obj): Axes object of subplot (1,2,3,.. [1,1,],[1,2]... etc.)
    data_in (ndarray): 2D data array.
    name (string): Name of plotted data.
    axes (tuple): Time and height 1D arrays.
    nc_file (str): Input file.

classes.name: mwrpy.plots.generate_plots.Dimensions

modules.name: mwrpy.cli
documentation:
A wrapper script for calling data processing functions.
All modules MUST have an add_arguments function
which adds the subcommand to the subparser.
!/usr/bin/env python3

modules.name: mwrpy.atmos
documentation: Module for atmsopheric functions.

functions.name: mwrpy.atmos.spec_heat
documentation: Specific heat for evaporation (J/kg).

functions.name: mwrpy.atmos.vap_pres
documentation: Water vapor pressure (Pa).

functions.name: mwrpy.atmos.t_dew_rh
documentation: Dew point temperature (K) from relative humidity ().

functions.name: mwrpy.atmos.pot_tem
documentation: Potential temperature (K).

functions.name: mwrpy.atmos.eq_pot_tem
documentation: Equivalent potential temperature (K).

functions.name: mwrpy.atmos.rel_hum
documentation: Relative humidity ().

functions.name: mwrpy.atmos.rh_err
documentation:
Calculates relative humidity error propagation
from absolute humidity and temperature ().

functions.name: mwrpy.atmos.abs_hum
documentation: Absolute humidity (kg/m^3).

functions.name: mwrpy.atmos.calc_p_baro
documentation: Calculate pressure (Pa) in each level using barometric height formula.

functions.name: mwrpy.atmos.calc_saturation_vapor_pressure
documentation:
Goff-Gratch formula for saturation vapor pressure (Pa) over water adopted by WMO.

Args:
    temperature: Temperature (K).

Returns:
    Saturation vapor pressure (Pa).

functions.name: mwrpy.atmos.c2k
documentation: Converts Celsius to Kelvins.

functions.name: mwrpy.atmos.dir_avg
documentation: Computes average wind direction (DEG) for a certain window length.

functions.name: mwrpy.atmos.winddir
documentation: Computes mean wind direction (deg).

functions.name: mwrpy.atmos.find_lwcl_free
documentation:
Identifying liquid water cloud free periods using 31.4 GHz TB variability.
Uses water vapor channel as proxy for a humidity dependent threshold.

modules.name: mwrpy.level2.write_lev2_nc
documentation: Module for writing Level 2 netCDF files.

functions.name: mwrpy.level2.write_lev2_nc.lev2_to_nc
documentation:
This function reads Level 1 files,
applies retrieval coefficients for Level 2 products
and writes it into a netCDF file.

Args:
    data_type: Data type of the netCDF file.
    lev1_file: Path of Level 1 file.
    output_file: Name of output file.
    site: Name of site.
    temp_file: Name of temperature product file.
    hum_file: Name of humidity product file.
    coeff_files: List of coefficient files.

functions.name: mwrpy.level2.write_lev2_nc.get_products
documentation: Derive specified Level 2 products.

functions.name: mwrpy.level2.write_lev2_nc._combine_lev1
documentation: Add level1 data.

functions.name: mwrpy.level2.write_lev2_nc._del_att
documentation: Remove lev1 only attributes.

functions.name: mwrpy.level2.write_lev2_nc.load_product
documentation: Load existing lev2 file for deriving other products.

functions.name: mwrpy.level2.write_lev2_nc.ele_retrieval
documentation: Extracts elevation angles used in retrieval.

functions.name: mwrpy.level2.write_lev2_nc.retrieval_input
documentation: Get retrieval input.
